title: Coconut Semolina with Caramelised Onions & Chickpeas
description: Semolina cooked in coconut milk, crowned with a heap of slow-caramelised onions and spiced chickpeas — a generous, fragrant bowl that works as a main or a hearty side.

ingredients.name: Fresh coriander or parsley, to serve

steps: Peel and thinly slice all the onions. Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a wide pan over medium-low heat and cook the onions for 25-30 minutes, stirring every few minutes, until deeply golden and jammy. Be patient — this is the heart of the dish.

Add the chickpeas to the onions with 1 teaspoon of paprika and the cumin. Season with half the salt and cook together for 5 minutes until the chickpeas are coated and slightly crispy at the edges. Set aside.

In a saucepan, bring the coconut milk, water, remaining paprika and remaining salt to a gentle simmer. Pour in the semolina in a steady stream, stirring constantly. Cook over low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring, until the semolina is thick and pulls away from the sides. Cover and leave to steam off the heat for 2 minutes.
Spoon the semolina into bowls or onto a large platter, top generously with the caramelised onions and spiced chickpeas, and finish with fresh coriander or parsley and a drizzle of olive oil.
footnote: The onions need time — rushing them over high heat gives you stewed onions, not caramelised ones. Low and slow is the only way. A splash of water halfway through prevents them catching without losing colour.
